meta.title: Lev SDK × FlowMind × Poly × Mesh Kickoff
meta.description: Lev SDK hardening kickoff grounded in parity, graph, FlowMind, Poly, AgentPing, tmux, .now GenUI, ACP/WS, endpoint providers, evals, and constraint engineering.

props.title: Lev SDK, FlowMind, Poly Surfaces, and Mesh
props.subtitle: The product target is an operated adaptive agent runtime: a .now GenUI page can launch an interview, ACP/WS back into Lev, execute through SDK → FlowMind → PolyCard → tmux or endpoint provider, and stream receipts/evals back to the UI.

props.content: Claude, Cursor, Flue, Sandcastle, Mngr, AionUI, OpenAI Symphony, PR204/iii/XHawk, NAAC, and the graph parity corpus converge on one target: Lev as an adaptive agent runtime SDK. The hardening spine is SessionHandle + RunHandle + InteractionPoint + CapabilityCard + EvalSuite, backed by FlowMind gates, Poly capability projection, tmux/exec runtime, AgentPing action surfaces, OpenAI-compatible providers, and graph/event receipts.

graph LR
  SDK[SDK intent] --> FM[FlowMind policy/control]
  FM --> POLY[PolyCard / PolyOp RegistryRuntime]
  POLY --> BD[BackendDescriptor]
  BD --> TMUX[tmux AgentSession]
  TMUX --> EVT[LevEvent / PolyFrame stream]
  EVT --> REC[PolyReceipt + graph memory]
  REC --> MESH[Future mesh node reputation]
props.caption: Do not create a second runtime. The SDK projects into the same execution chain as CLI and MCP.

props.title: Known cutover bug
props.content: `core/sdk/src/index.ts` currently mirrors `substrate` into `host`, and `core/sdk/src/contract-matrix.ts` maps `substrate_policy` to CLI `host`. The next implementation slice must hard-cut `tmux://` to substrate-only.

props.label: External parity
props.title: What the market is telling us

rows.proves: Embeddable coding-agent loop with tools, sessions, permissions, hooks, subagents, MCP, checkpointing, usage, and OTel.
rows.lev: Match ergonomics but stay provider-agnostic and FlowMind/Poly owned.

rows.proves: IDE agent runtime is becoming local/cloud programmable infrastructure with streaming, cancellation, indexing, MCP, skills, hooks, and durable agents.
rows.lev: Make attach/resume, local-first operation, cloud promotion, and telemetry table stakes.

rows.proves: A TypeScript harness can own prompt/skill/shell/task primitives and deploy as CLI or HTTP with virtual sandbox support.
rows.lev: Let projects bind providers and sandboxes through PolyCard instead of hand-coded adapters.

rows.proves: Agent as Unix primitive: addressable processes with connect, message, capture, wait, push/pull/pair, snapshots, events, labels, and local/remote providers.
rows.lev: Use this as the session ergonomics bar; keep provider truth in PolyCard and receipts in Graph.

rows.proves: Task tracker as control plane: every open task maps to an isolated agent workspace, agents run continuously, and work reaches workflow-defined handoff states.
rows.lev: Workstream/task is durable identity; session is the live control handle. tmux:// stays canonical for CLI-backed runs; app-server/cloud APIs become provider cards later.

rows.proves: Operator shell/control-plane surface: cowork dashboard, remote channels, pair approvals, ACP/MCP readiness, team session coordination, scheduled tasks, and headless/WebUI/mobile affordances.
rows.lev: Extract into AgentPing, platform channels, Poly provider readiness, scheduling, and Team/FlowMind projections; reject Electron shell ownership, YOLO approvals, and SQLite runtime state.

rows.proves: Worker/Function/Trigger as backend primitives with live discovery and capability registration.
rows.lev: Map Worker -> PolyCard/provider, Function -> PolyOp, Trigger -> FlowMind/EventBus. Extract live binding, do not vendor iii.

rows.proves: Session history as audit substrate: git push captures session<->commit linkage into XDG events; session list/for-commit/context rehydrates future agents.
rows.lev: Promote event/receipt/session-context patterns after tests; make retention policy-driven and explicit.

rows.proves: Provider/sandbox/worktree/session/recovery contracts are useful independent of a graph runtime.
rows.lev: Extract contracts; keep FlowMind semantics and PolyCard truth.

rows.proves: Stateful graph workflows need durable execution, streaming, HITL, memory, tracing, and deployment.
rows.lev: Compete at orchestration, differentiate with graph patches, tmux/VFS, receipts, and mesh economics.

rows.proves: Tool/context projection is standardized around client-server JSON-RPC.
rows.lev: Generate MCP from PolyOp where safe; do not make MCP execution or mesh.

props.title: 1. Launch
props.content: SDK/CLI/MCP starts an arch interview or FlowMind run with `substrate: 'tmux://...'`.

props.title: 2. Resolve
props.content: FlowMind profile and PolyCard registry resolve a BackendDescriptor before runtime.

props.title: 3. Operate
props.content: tmux AgentSession owns live CLI lifecycle. Humans can attach and kick the tires.

props.title: 4. Stream
props.content: SDK receives text/tool/usage/context-pressure/checkpoint events as LevEvent/PolyFrame projections.

props.title: 5. Control
props.content: Session handle supports `wait`, `cancel`, `attach`, `capture`, and typed receipts.

props.title: 6. Remember
props.content: Graph records receipts, provenance, relationships, and future mesh reputation signals.

props.title: Boundary
props.content: `sdk.agent.session()` should feel like Claude/Cursor/Flue, but it must be a facade over `@lev-os/exec`, not a second runtime owner.

rows.rule: Resolve through project > user > global; override only where allowed.

props.label: Surfaces
props.title: Generated parity with explicit trap doors

rows.default: Full authoring and runtime session API
rows.trap: Advanced stream transforms and custom validators.

rows.default: Human/operator commands over common SDK ops
rows.trap: Omit complex object args; add sugar like attach/session commands.
Only after manifest/proto tier is stable.

props.label: Graph OS
props.title: Graph, VFS, reactive hooks, and mesh
Graph = truth/proof
props.content: Stores memory, entities, relationships, receipts, and projections. It does not replace the scheduler.

props.title: CodeGraph = plugin projection
props.content: Useful for code intel and prior-art lookup, but not canonical runtime truth.

props.title: AgentFS/VFS = audit substrate
props.content: Promote after session API is stable; use as write-gate and recoverable sandbox layer.

props.title: Mesh = long-term market
props.content: Receipts and PolyCard identity become trust, routing, reputation, and settlement primitives.

items.title: Fix SDK substrate/host cutover
items.description: Remove aliasing, repair contract matrix, add tests around `tmux://` substrate.

items.title: Session ergonomics
items.description: Add `sdk.agent.session()` with stream, wait, cancel, attach, capture, receipt.

items.title: GenUI/AgentPing action host
items.description: Let a .now/GenUI surface start, answer, approve, stream, and regenerate through typed ACP/WS action intents over the SDK session handle.

items.title: FlowMind authoring in code
items.description: Add `sdk.flow.define()` DSL that emits FlowMind/Poly declarations and proves parity with YAML path.

items.title: Eval/constraint SDK
items.description: Add Jest-like sdk.eval suites that round-trip with FlowMind/DNA and emit deterministic + adversarial receipts.

items.title: PolyCard project binding
items.description: Let projects declare providers/capabilities and generate SDK/CLI/MCP clients with projection policy.

items.title: Sandbox provider cards
items.description: Keep tmux first; add bind-mount/container/cloud/VFS cards later.

props.label: Roadmap
props.title: If parity and VFS are table stakes, then what?

props.label: 1 year
props.title: Local-first agent OS
props.content: Every project binds capabilities through Poly and runs FlowMind-controlled agents through SDK/CLI/MCP with tmux-visible sessions, receipts, attach/resume, VFS audit, and generated docs.

props.label: 5 years
props.title: Federated personal/team mesh
props.content: Lev installs advertise PolyCards, trust/lease policy, compute, locality, sovereignty, and receipt history. Work routes across trusted nodes with human governance.

props.label: 10 years
props.title: Verified cognition market
props.content: FlowMind programs become reproducible experiments. Receipts prove work, cost, context, policy, and outcomes. Reputation, verifier quorums, and model/provider markets sit on top.

rows.decision: Should the next public API be `sdk.agent.session()` or should `sdk.agent.run()` return a handle?
rows.recommendation: Add `session()` and keep `run()` as one-shot convenience.

rows.decision: Should `host` disappear or survive only as machine/location?
rows.recommendation: Hard-cut `tmux://` to substrate; reserve `host` for location only.
Design now, build after session ergonomics.

rows.decision: Which session controls can MCP expose?
rows.recommendation: Read/list/status/capture first; guarded write controls later.
VFS/write-gate for Lev moat unless customer needs container isolation.
Do not build mesh in MVP; encode receipts and identity now.

rows.decision: Should GenUI drive SDK execution or only render SDK-declared actions?
rows.recommendation: Render only; actions return as typed SDK/PolyOp invocation intents.

rows.decision: Should AionUI become Lev shell/runtime or parity extraction source?
rows.recommendation: Extract operator-surface/channel/provider-readiness/team-mode patterns; keep Lev runtime canon in SDK/Exec/FlowMind/Poly/Event/Graph.

rows.decision: Should iii-style live Worker/Function/Trigger binding block tmux/session MVP?
rows.recommendation: No. Record as next dispatch-loop promotion after lev exec + tmux session proof.

rows.decision: Should createEventDispatch(config) be locked as the public API name?
rows.recommendation: No. Keep the module ownership decision; treat the factory name as branch evidence to iterate during interview.

rows.decision: Should SDK target only workflows or also adaptive agent runtime?
rows.recommendation: Both. Workflow can be one step; FlowMind also acts as the gate/policy envelope around adaptive sessions.
Supported inbound/peer protocol edge, not core interaction model or runtime dependency.
Yes, but only by emitting typed ACP/AgentPing actions; SDK/Poly executes and returns receipts.
Yes as provider/capability cards beside tmux CLI providers, with usage/eval receipts and no bespoke adapter branches.
Design now and ship a thin v1 early; evals/constraints are half the SDK and NAAC is the proof.

props.content:
Local artifacts: `.lev/pm/designs/design-sdk-flowmind-poly-mesh-kickoff.md`, `.lev/pm/parity/sandcastle.yaml`, `/Users/jean-patricksmith/digital/leviathan/.lev/pm/parity/aionui.yaml`, `/Users/jean-patricksmith/digital/leviathan/.lev/pm/parity/openai-symphony.yaml`, `workshop/analysis/sandcastle/analysis.md`, `workshop/analysis/infranodus-video-1/analysis.md`, `docs/specs/spec-agentping.md`, `docs/specs/spec-levui-ir.md`, `docs/specs/spec-genui-provider-model.md`, `core/domain/src/poly-protocol/card.ts`, `core/sdk/src/index.ts`, and `core/sdk/src/contract-matrix.ts`.

Remote branch evidence: `origin/claude/recreate-xhawk-features-Sj8i4` files `workshop/analysis/iii/analysis.md`, `workshop/analysis/halo/analysis.md`, `mvp.md`, and SDLC session-history handlers.

External refs: Claude Agent SDK, Cursor SDK, Flue, Sandcastle, LangGraph, MCP architecture docs, and official OpenAI Symphony/harness engineering posts.

Parity corpus note: the main checkout parity folder is the strongest current market/prior-art input; this worktree lags some rows, so API freeze should read `/Users/jean-patricksmith/digital/leviathan/.lev/pm/parity/` first.

Additional scan artifacts: `.lev/pm/workstreams/workshop-intake/captures/20260502-capture-sweep-sdk-surface-parity-graph.yaml`, `.lev/pm/captures/20260420-graph-parity-convergence-synthesis.yaml`, and `workshop/analysis/comparisons/graph-flowmind-sim/overlap.minimum-set.yaml`.

Newly folded caveats: external NAAC flows at `/Users/jean-patricksmith/digital/kingly/apps/production/naac/.lev/flows/naac-anticheat.flow.yaml`, `naac-anticheat-100.flow.yaml`, `naac-final-boss.flow.yaml`, `naac-ralph.flow.yaml`, and `naac-constraint-iterative.flow.yaml`; endpoint provider evidence at `core/harness/src/providers/ai-sdk.ts`; eval evidence at `plugins/autoresearch/src/engine/eval-harness.ts`, `plugins/harness-patterns/src/guardrails/types.ts`, and `plugins/sdlc/flows/eval-harness.flow.yaml`.

graph LR
  SDK[SDK actions + session schema] --> STREAM[LevEvent / PolyFrame stream]
  STREAM --> IR[LevUI IR / GenUI spec]
  IR --> HOST[AgentPing or host surface]
  HOST --> ACTION[Typed human/model action intent]
  ACTION --> POLY[SDK / PolyOp invocation]
  POLY --> RECEIPT[PolyReceipt + workstream graph]
props.caption: The UI is a projection of SDK-declared actions and runtime events. It is not a second runtime.

rows.role: Execution/invocation bundle over @lev-os/exec; typed sessions, actions, streams, receipts, generated clients.
rows.rule: No renderer logic; no hidden runtime fallback.

rows.role: Presentation projection: deterministic rendering of reports, dashboards, approvals, and session state from validated IR/specs.
rows.rule: No business/runtime logic; emit typed action intents only.

rows.role: Abstract action-surface contract for approvals, questions, selections, notifications, progress, leases, and intervention across dashboard/phone/CLI/future hosts.
rows.rule: Not just dashboard UI; route typed interaction points and return typed intents.

rows.role: Concrete operator-shell prior art: cowork dashboard, channel adapters, pair approvals, ACP/MCP readiness, team mode, scheduled tasks, and remote/headless control surfaces.
rows.rule: Extract as AgentPing/platforms/Poly/scheduling projections over Lev state; reject runtime shell ownership, YOLO approvals, and SQLite as canon.
Admission and policy before execution; no renderer ownership.

rows.role: Durable institutional identity across many sessions.
rows.rule: Regulated/proof-bearing runs attach sessions to workstreams and receipts.

props.title: Recommended doc label
props.content: Use “schema-first surface contract” as the canonical GenUI↔SDK phrase. AionUI supplies the concrete operator-shell patterns; Symphony supplies the run-lifecycle patterns.

props.label: Parity synthesis
props.title: AionUI + Symphony divide the control plane cleanly
Symphony = run lifecycle
props.content: Tracker-bound candidate selection, claim/run/retry/continuation, deterministic workspaces, app-server turns, and proof-of-work bundles.

props.title: AionUI = operator shell
props.content: Cowork dashboard, channels, approvals, provider readiness, MCP config projection, team roster/task board/mailbox, and remote/headless visibility.

props.title: Shared Lev contracts
props.content: AgentPing active-run projection, AgentGuard leases, Poly provider/tool metadata, and LevEvent envelopes for run/tool/approval/channel/dashboard updates.

props.title: Extraction boundary
props.content: Do not adopt Electron shell ownership, YOLO/full-auto semantics, or process-owned SQLite state. Those weaken Lev graph/event/policy canon.

props.label: Parity deep dive
props.title: The actual contracts and ergonomics we bought

rows.extract: Durable Agent/workstream separated from per-prompt Run; terminal cancellation; archive/delete; pagination; typed error taxonomy.
session.prompt/shell/skill/task/delete, default and named sessions, persistent history, compaction events.
One object that streams typed events and supports interrupt/cancel/model/permission control plus resumable stream tokens.
Text/tool/task/command/hook/usage/compaction/idle/error event union rather than stdout-only streams.
rows.source: Claude cas-10, cas-11; Flue flue-15, flue-33, flue-34

rows.extract: Print/interactive command contract, stream parser, session capture, usage parser, env merge, provider health.
Bind-mount/isolated/no-sandbox taxonomy; worktree lifecycle; locks; copy/sync; recovery bundles; git identity.
Create/destroy/list/watch, attach, message injection, capture scrollback, observe/notify, push/pull/pair, snapshot, wait, exec, idle policy.
Cowork dashboard, provider picker, pair approvals, remote tool confirmation, team coordinator, channel lifecycle, mobile/headless host.
ACP as inbound/peer protocol plus MCP/HTTP/WS/gRPC/CLI projections; protocols do not own the interaction model.
rows.source: AionUI aionui-03, 04; GasTown gt-24; Claude cas-09

rows.extract: Candidate dispatch, claim/running/retry/release, reconciliation, continuation turns, deterministic workspace, proof-of-work bundle.

props.title: SDK freeze implication
props.content: Do not freeze around runAgent(prompt). Freeze around SessionHandle, RunHandle, InteractionPoint, and CapabilityCard; everything else is a projection or adapter.

props.label: Adaptive runtime
props.title: Workflow includes declared DAGs, one-shot runs, and bounded agent autonomy
Declared workflow
props.content: FlowMind graph/YAML or sdk.flow.define(); topology is known before execution; gates enforce node transitions.

props.title: One-shot run
props.content: sdk.agent.run() or one-node FlowMind; provider/workspace/tool/result gates still apply.

props.title: Adaptive session
props.content: Agent explores within constraints; Lev enforces at leases, tool calls, filesystem writes, tests, budgets, stop conditions, and receipts.

props.title: Design caveat
props.content: FlowMind is not only static workflow DSL. It is also the policy/gate envelope around adaptive agent runtime behavior.

props.label: Graph deep dive
props.title: What the graph corpus actually changes

rows.meaning: Prompt context export, living docs, dashboards, browser/static views.
rows.sdk: Sessions and plugin workflows need projection hooks and graph-backed evidence bundles.

rows.meaning: Selector grammar, BFS/query, summarization, graph-context export.
rows.sdk: Expose retrieval policy and context assembly without hardcoding it into agents.

rows.meaning: LongMemEval-style tests, hypothesis suites, shell CI, graph fixtures.
rows.sdk: Every workflow promotion needs falsifiable fixtures and receipt-backed verification.

rows.meaning: Repo-state handoffs, worktrees, review queues, dashboards.
rows.sdk: Workstream/session identity must survive runs and support resume/attach/replay.

rows.meaning: Immutable artifacts, integrity chains, context.yaml/living-doc projections.
rows.sdk: Receipts and artifacts are first-class outputs, not incidental files.

rows.meaning: E-graphs/equality saturation for FlowMind IR canonicalization.
rows.sdk: Keep as bounded sidecar; do not replace core graph truth.

rows.meaning: Live simulation/world surfaces are far-lane projection targets.
rows.sdk: Do not block v1, but preserve graph/event/receipt semantics so this can grow later.

props.title: Graph conclusion
props.content: The corpus does not ask for a new graph kernel. It asks for projection/query/operator lanes over append-only graph/event truth.

props.label: End-state
props.title: Run the interview from a .now GenUI page

props.content:
sequenceDiagram
  participant UI as .now GenUI page
  participant ACP as ACP/WS ingress
  participant AP as AgentPing/action host
  participant SDK as @lev-os/sdk
  participant FM as FlowMind policy
  participant POLY as PolyCard/PolyOp
  participant RT as tmux:// or endpoint provider
  participant EVT as LevEvent/Receipt stream
  UI->>ACP: typed action / answer / approval
  ACP->>AP: protocol frame
  AP->>SDK: InteractionPoint invocation
  SDK->>FM: admitted session request
  FM->>POLY: resolve provider/card/op
  POLY->>RT: BackendDescriptor + policy
  RT-->>EVT: text/tool/usage/eval/checkpoint
  EVT-->>UI: WS projection + regenerated UI state
props.caption: .now is the surface; ACP is an ingress/peer protocol; SDK/FlowMind/Poly own execution and receipts.

props.title: Browser/WASM rule
props.content: Browser FlowMind/WASM can run previews, local inference, and UI-side preflight, but durable execution/admission uses the same event + receipt schema once it touches project state.

props.label: Providers
props.title: CLI/tmux and OpenAI-compatible endpoints are the same SDK family

rows.current: tmux:// is canonical for CLI because scrollback, attach/intervene, manual resume, and audit history are the product requirement.
rows.sdk: Session handles resolve to tmux AgentSession for CLI providers; no direct hidden subprocess fallback for proof-bearing runs.

rows.current: core/harness/src/providers/ai-sdk.ts supports Vercel AI SDK plus OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions for named providers like Bonsai/OpenRouter.
rows.sdk: Endpoint providers become provider/capability cards with usage normalization and health/config gates.

rows.current: kingly/cloud and ai-sdk prior art point toward text, image, video, voice, and local/remote gateways.
rows.sdk: Text/image/video/voice enter through capability metadata and eval receipts, not bespoke adapter branches.

rows.current: LangGraph raises the bar for durable workflows, streaming, HITL, memory, tracing, and deployment.
rows.sdk: Lev competes via FlowMind + Poly + receipts; endpoint providers are just one substrate behind the same session/eval API.

props.title: Provider rule
props.content: Use provider cards and capabilities. Avoid random adapter code, compatibility wrappers, hidden fallbacks, or provider-specific business logic.

props.label: Evals
props.title: Evals and constraint engineering are the other half of the SDK

rows.repo: plugins/autoresearch/src/engine/eval-harness.ts implements evaluate(input) -> EvalResult and comparison-ready outcomes.
rows.api: sdk.eval needs strategy adapters, result envelopes, comparison, and run receipts.

rows.repo: plugins/harness-patterns/src/guardrails/types.ts models block, warn, require-approval, gate, and log actions.
rows.api: Constraints should compile to deterministic gates and HITL/AgentGuard decisions.
SDK eval suites must round-trip to FlowMind/DNA and back.

rows.repo: NAAC shows code-first constraints are easier while grammar settles.
rows.api: Developer writes expect-like constraints, then gets FlowMind/DNA/CLI/MCP projections.

props.title: Fail-closed rule
props.content: An eval run without receipts, explicit gates, or deterministic proof is not complete. Constraint suites are promotion gates, not dashboard decoration.

props.title: Deterministic probes
props.content: naac-anticheat scans for custom normalization, mock boundary cheating, reverse imports, raw scenario loaders, and FMM fail-closed regressions.

props.title: Adversarial model review
props.content: Agentic scan checks canned outputs, verdict smoothing, conditional overrides, and other cheats grep cannot catch.

props.title: Final boss gate
props.content: naac-final-boss runs preflight, deterministic system validation, adversarial review, then accepts or rejects. No retry loop inside the terminal gate.

props.title: Ralph constraint loop
props.content: naac-ralph runs final boss, finds the reddest invariant, fixes one thing, and reruns until green or blocked.

props.title: SDK lesson
props.content: Every discovered exploit becomes a permanent constraint. sdk.eval must be peer to sdk.agent and sdk.flow.
